projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: [dog_run] The camera experienced a brief period of no video frames.

There is a strong pattern of increased activity centered around the backyard and front entrance (18:57 - 19:03), including doorbell rings, dog barking, and the arrival of an unknown person (white shirt) shortly after Steve is observed working in the backyard. This suggests a significant, unobserved visitor or event that is generating noise and movement, which is unusual for a typical weekday evening.
Unusually high activity at backyard this hour: 5 observations vs typical 1 for weekday 19:00
[front_yard] The front yard camera is experiencing persistent connection failure and signal loss.
[front_yard] The front_yard camera has been non-functional for approximately 20 minutes.
[front_yard] The front_yard camera is non-functional and displaying an error message indicating that no video frames are being received.

There is a concerning pattern of escalating, unobserved activity at the front of the house between 17:57 and 18:02, indicated by urgent dog barking and multiple doorbell rings. This external audio disturbance is not matched by any visible activity on the front door or entryway cameras, suggesting a potential visitor or repeated disturbance that is either passing quickly or is not being captured by the visual sensors.

Although an unexpected voice was detected at 16:05, the subsequent activity logs (16:34 onwards) show the household is fully occupied and settled, with multiple devices online and family members moving through the house. This suggests the initial audio alert was likely a false alarm or related to a brief, unseen visitor, and the home is currently secure and occupied.
[front_yard] The front_yard camera is currently non-functional, preventing any monitoring of the front yard, driveway, or street.
[entryway] An unexpected voice was recently detected by audio sensors, but no person is visible in the camera's view.
